notes: Twenty-sixth validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its mission says the school provides a challenging, individualized education program with a moral focus.
Official materials say the school organizes its core values under the acronym R.A.I.S.E.
The school says parent involvement is the heartbeat of its campus culture.
Official materials say the founder is Hilda Alston Parler, who previously served as the first board president.
Official pages list Krista Wyman as director of 7-12.
The school says it operates as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit overseen by an independent board of directors.
Its mission says the school builds on the Core Knowledge Sequence and educates students through the trivium of grammar, logic, and rhetoric.
Official materials say the school has served as a college preparatory option for its community for over twenty years.
The school explicitly identifies itself as a Challenge Foundation Academy.
Official pages quote Thomas Jefferson: a society that wishes to be both ignorant and free wants what never was and never will be.
The school's overview says it follows the classical education model through the three natural stages of human development: grammar, logic, and rhetoric.
A recent middle school homepage note says Kristilee Newton continues as Grammar School Principal.
Official board materials show the school uses the acronym WSA.
Official materials say Dustin Richardson serves as board chair.
Official materials list Mel Rausch as board treasurer.
Official materials list Sarah Dawson as board secretary.
Official board pages include Kevin Lee-y Green as member at large and identify him as founder of Techmoja Dance and Theater Company.
Official board pages note that Kevin Lee-y Green studied contemporary dance and ballet at the University of North Carolina School of the Arts.
Its mission says the school prepares students to become outstanding leaders and motivates them to strive for their greatest potential through exceptional character and academic achievements.
The school's vision says it aims to be one of the most successful schools in the world, measured by service to the community and student outcomes.
Official materials include a student pledge with the lines, "I will LEARN. I will GROW. I will PROSPER."
The school highlights a 21st Century Community Learning Center program with components such as WE EXCEL, STEM activities, and Positive Action.
Official program materials say character and leadership are promoted through weekly activities and expanded learning opportunities.
The school's handbook says success is measured partly by helping students graduate, obtain successful careers, and become model citizens of the world.
Official materials identify the school by the name STARS Charter.
The school's official public materials are currently too sparse to support higher-quality, school-specific clue writing in this batch.
